column1: What is a section in an object/executable file?
column2: A named region holding one kind of content (.text, .data, .bss, ...). In firmware you must understand and often control which physical memory each one lands in.

column1: Two deciding questions that place any byte into a section.
column2: Does it change at run time? (flash vs RAM) and does it need a stored initial value? (in the image vs merely reserved). These sort everything into .text, .rodata, .data, or .bss.

column1: What does the .text section hold and where does it live?
column2: Executable code (and sometimes constants). It lives in flash and is stored in the image, executing in place.
const data and string literals. Lives in flash, stored in the image (read-only, runs in place).
Globals/statics with a nonzero initial value. They run from RAM (VMA) but their initial values are stored in flash (LMA) and copied to RAM at boot.
Globals/statics initialised to zero (or uninitialised). Live in RAM, with nothing stored in the image — just space reserved and zeroed at boot.

column1: What is the difference between LMA and VMA?
column2: LMA (Load Memory Address) is where the bytes sit in the image (flash). VMA (Virtual/run Memory Address) is where the program expects them at run time (RAM). They differ for .data.

column1: Which sections have LMA = VMA, and which differs?
column2: .text and .rodata have LMA = VMA (they run in place from flash). .data has VMA in RAM but LMA in flash (copied at boot). .bss has no LMA at all — only zeroing.

column1: What does the .ld MEMORY block declare?
column2: The chip's physical memories, e.g. FLASH (rx) ORIGIN = 0x08000000 LENGTH = 256K and RAM (rwx) ORIGIN = 0x20000000 LENGTH = 64K.

column1: What does the linker phrase > RAM AT> FLASH mean?
column2: Run the section from RAM (VMA in RAM) but store its bytes in flash (LMA in FLASH) — the VMA/LMA split made explicit, used for .data.

column1: What three things does the reset handler do before calling main?
column2: Copy .data initial values from flash (LMA) to RAM (VMA), zero the .bss region, then (set up FPU/clocks/C++ constructors) and finally call main().

column1: Who actually makes int x = 5 equal 5 and int y equal 0 at startup?
column2: The startup code: copying .data from flash gives x its 5, and zeroing .bss gives y its 0. C guarantees statics start at zero, and the reset handler is who does it.

column1: Why are bad startup/linker bugs so maddening?
column2: Initialised globals contain garbage and zero-init globals are nonzero, yet the program may seem to run. The C standard promised the initialisation, so the failure is invisible in the source.

column1: How do you place a variable in a custom section?
column2: __attribute__((section("..."))) on the symbol, with the linker script mapping that section — e.g. a buffer in fast CCM RAM, or a NOINIT/.noinit variable the startup code is told not to zero so it survives a warm reset.
